findings.title: Bacteria nowcast beats station-memory status quo
findings.headline: AP 0.5099 vs 0.2782 · dAP +0.2317
findings.plain: On the EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO 2022+ deployable stratum, the calibrated bacteria model beats the station-memory baseline with a clustered-bootstrap AP margin whose CI excludes zero.
findings.how: Frozen temporal holdout, n=89,321 / 8,999 events, compared against a station-memory baseline and checked by decision-curve net benefit.
findings.note: This is the stronger public bacteria headline than the stale pooled/Monterey-only table row.

findings.title: Harmonic tide range adds a small pooled bacteria lift
findings.headline: dAP +0.0091 over rain+discharge
findings.plain: The spring-neap/tide-table feature has a real pooled temporal lift, but the broader regional-transfer story was later softened by the expanded holdout audit.
findings.how: Claim-card margin test plus later H1 trust-index caveat: pooled lift holds; robust across-region generalization does not yet clear the bar.
findings.note: Keep as a caveated positive, not as a broad deployed feature claim.

findings.title: Clean-to-dirty beach surprise reframe passes retrospective gate
findings.headline: AP 0.4177 vs station-memory 0.199
findings.plain: Among currently-clean beach-days, the frozen statewide model predicts clean-to-dirty exceedance surprises far better than station memory.
findings.how: Same frozen bacteria model, EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO, reveal-lag discipline, evaluated on the onset slice instead of the already-dirty persistence slice.
findings.note: Final breakthrough claim remains blocked until the post-2026-06-18 prospective lockbox has rows and is scored.

findings.title: Adaptive sampling replay beats site-memory sampling
findings.headline: top-20 capture 0.7062 vs 0.5299
findings.plain: Under the same sampling budget, model-ranked sampling catches more exceedances than station-memory ranking in retrospective replay.
findings.how: Ranking replay and full mixed-arm weekly protocol replay on frozen bacteria scores.
findings.note: Ops-validation ready; not yet a standalone prospective discovery claim.

findings.title: Bacteria breakthrough gate is retrospective-pass, prospective-blocked
findings.headline: PASS · BLOCKED_PROSPECTIVE_LOCKBOX
findings.plain: The operational, onset, and spatial checks pass retrospectively, but the final breakthrough label is blocked by an empty prospective lockbox.
findings.how: Breakthrough gate reads FROZEN_HEADLINE plus onset and spatial holdout metrics, then enforces the post-freeze lockbox rule.
findings.note: FROZEN_HEADLINE.json has scored_forward=false; score post-freeze rows before claiming final breakthrough.

findings.title: CatBoost has a real ranking edge over frozen HGBT
findings.headline: AP 0.5164 vs 0.5082 · CI [0.0041, 0.0193]
findings.plain: The CatBoost champion-upgrade candidate beats the frozen HGBT ranking metric with a clustered-bootstrap margin that excludes zero.
findings.how: Same EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO temporal holdout; decision-curve analysis then checks whether the ranking edge changes operations.
findings.note: RECORD-DON'T-SHIP (operationally) — the AP margin is real and county-robust (clustered CI [0.0041, 0.0193] excludes 0), but at the advisory threshold the net-benefit gain is NEGLIGIBLE (max |delta| 0.00138 < 0.002). The ranking edge is concentrated in the dry/summer slices but does not translate into deployment value, so a frozen-champion swap + new dependency is not justified on operational grounds. The honest case to ship rests on calibration (ECE) + the dry/summer-slice gain, not net benefit.

findings.title: LightGBM and CatBoost both clear the paired HGBT test
findings.headline: CatBoost +0.0132; LightGBM +0.0081
findings.plain: Two independent tree learners beat frozen HGBT in paired AP tests, confirming the model ceiling is slightly above the old HGBT run.
findings.how: Paired bootstrap plus leave-one-county-out win counts on the same 89,321-row deployable stratum.
findings.note: Registry still keeps them as benchmark/champion-upgrade candidates pending freeze/reproduction and operational-value gates.

findings.title: Frozen bacteria model passes held-from-fitting 2026 season
findings.headline: AP 0.6335 vs station-memory 0.3682
findings.plain: On the locked 2026-01-05 to 2026-03-05 held-from-fitting season, the frozen model beats the strongest baseline and retains discrimination.
findings.how: D3 holdout keys are hash-pinned; F1 scores the frozen model against persistence, seasonal, and station-memory baselines.
findings.note: These 2026-01-05..2026-03-05 rows are inside the frozen TEST split (>=2022) and were ALREADY SCORED by the published headline; they were never trained/calibrated/gated on. This is a HELD-FROM-FITTING forward season, not a pristine never-scored lockbox. The pristine post-2026-06-18 lockbox is EMPTY (data ends 2026-03-05).

findings.title: Bacteria forecast horizon is honest to about 2 days
findings.headline: skill over station-memory through lead 2d; lead 3d loses
findings.plain: The corrected horizon claim compares against a multi-feature station-memory model, not the weak single-bit persistence baseline.
findings.how: Lead-day sweep with reveal-lag discipline and paired AP margin over station-memory model.
findings.note: Current per-lead rows show L=2 is CI-separated over station-memory; L=3 is a loss. Do not claim a 3-day horizon.

findings.title: Deployable bacteria advisory rule catches most exceedances
findings.headline: recall 0.8645 · precision 0.2301
findings.plain: The calibrated decision rule turns the model into an advisory threshold chosen on validation, then read on the 2022+ test era.
findings.how: Validation-chosen threshold tau, then test operating-point and decision-curve readout.
findings.note: No fabricated dollar/illness counts. The trade-off is stated in operational units (exceedances caught vs false advisories) + the DCA-implied cost ratio. A site that wants fewer closures raises tau (toward the max-precision point); a site prioritising public health lowers it (toward max-detection). EPA recreational-water practice errs toward warning, i.e. a LOW tau, which this rule reflects.

findings.title: Russian River upstream turbidity adds lead-2 skill
findings.headline: dAP +0.0489 vs persistence
findings.plain: Upstream turbidity plus downstream persistence beats downstream persistence alone for the Guerneville intake turbidity event target.
findings.how: Leave-year-style out-of-fold lead sweep over Hopland/Digger Bend/Guerneville USGS IV turbidity.
findings.note: A narrow advection escape, not a general bacteria-beach driver claim.

findings.title: Cache Slough upstream lead sweep finds one new escape

findings.plain: A systematic USGS-IV upstream/downstream sweep found one new turbidity advection escape beyond the Russian River positive control.
findings.how: Screen thousands of gauge pairs, then confirm top candidates with bootstrap and permutation gates.
findings.note: Rare/special-case advection escape; possible tidal-phase artifact remains a caveat.

findings.title: Drivers matter on wet-tail bacteria extremes

findings.plain: Although drivers do not improve the pooled mean, they add strong, CI-separated skill on the wet/storm high-consequence tail.
findings.how: Driver-free versus driver-augmented HGBT contrast on exogenous train-derived p90 severity regimes.

findings.title: GRU sequence model beats HGBT at 14-28 day hypoxia lead

findings.plain: A genuine temporal GRU extracts multi-week hypoxia lead dynamics that the stratification HGBT misses at 14 and 28 days.
findings.how: Train <=2021, test >2021; station-block/bootstrap deltas against HGBT-strat and persistence.
findings.note: 7d and 21d are not CI-separated against HGBT; report the horizon-specific result.

findings.title: Hypoxia onset forecast beats better naive across horizons
findings.headline: 7d AP 0.835 vs naive 0.7474
findings.plain: On the currently-non-hypoxic origins, the stratification model beats the better seasonal/DO-proximity naive for 3-28 day onset windows.

findings.title: Tokyo Bay shadow probe finds multiple positive physical targets

findings.title: Tokyo Bay GPU miner finds bottom-chlorophyll signal

findings.note: Research-only/noncommercial Tokyo Bay data.

findings.title: mcyE genotype-to-toxin effect definitively replicates in Alberta
findings.headline: rho 0.6824 · n=1461 · 55 lakes
findings.plain: The toxic-genotype signal predicts microcystin beyond biomass across 55 lakes and 5 years in an independent Alberta bloom-monitoring corpus.
findings.how: Partial Spearman of mcyE versus microcystin controlling biomass and lake, with permutation and leave-one-lake/year robustness.
findings.note: Scope is detectable/bloom-regime samples; biomass is total-cyano cell count, not chlorophyll.

findings.title: mcyE genotype-to-toxin effect replicates nationally
findings.headline: rho 0.4423 · perm-p 0.0024
findings.plain: A national WQP paired corpus confirms mcyE absolute predicts microcystin beyond biomass across independent lakes and years.
findings.how: Partial Spearman with biomass control, permutation null, leave-one-site and leave-one-year checks.
findings.note: Fraction-with-biomass (Erie's +0.65) is NOT cleanly testable nationally: the WQP program carrying total-cyano lacks chlorophyll. mcyE-absolute (Erie +0.21) IS testable with biomass control -- that is test A.

findings.title: National microcystin driver-null holds

reports/hab/erie/ERIE_CYANO_DRIVER_BREAK.text:
# Western Lake Erie microcystin — does nutrient LOADING break the cyano driver-null?

**Two-Claude campaign, 2026-06-19. Verdict: NO. The cyano driver-null HOLDS — now tested
with the two levers the satellite work could never reach: real TOXIN labels and gold-standard
tributary nutrient LOADING flux.**

## Why this was the genuinely-open question
The national CyAN-satellite cyano work ([[algae-bloom-frontier]]) found bloom *occurrence* is
governed by satellite-CI autocorrelation + seasonal climatology, with no dynamic weather or
nutrient driver lifting skill once season + lake-climate were controlled. But CyAN cannot see
**microcystin toxin**, and no national feed carries **tributary nutrient loading**. The memory
named exactly these as the only untested levers for a real cyano breakthrough. Western Lake
Erie is the one system where both exist on disk:

- **GLERL/NOAA western-basin field sampling** (`glerl_lake_erie_hab_wq`): in-situ
  **particulate microcystin** + chlorophyll + phycocyanin + lake TP/SRP/N at the WE2–WE16
  transect, 2012–2019 (641 toxin obs; the 2020–2022 file layout drops station id).
- **Heidelberg Tributary Loading Program** (`heidelberg_htlp`): **Maumee River** near-daily
  flow + TP + SRP, ~500 samples/yr — the exact data NOAA's seasonal bloom-severity forecast
  is built on.

Western Lake Erie *Microcystis* is the textbook nutrient-driven HAB. If the driver-null was
going to break anywhere, it was here.

## What we tested (3 independent lanes)

**Lane A — loading FORECAST gate** (`research/hab/erie_microcystin_driver_gate.py`).
Target = next-visit particulate microcystin > 1.6 µg/L (Ohio/WHO advisory) at a WE station,
chained within-season (gap ≤ 24 d). Baseline = rich history + season + station (lagged
microcystin/chl/phyco, day-of-year, station). Driver = 22 antecedent **Maumee loading**
features (integrated TP & SRP load, flow, flow-weighted conc over 7/14/28/56 d + spring
cumulative). Eval = leave-one-YEAR-out AP (blooms cluster by year). Gates = ΔAP ≥ 0.005 +
perm-null + noise-column floor.

> Result: **ΔAP = −0.028** (loading *hurts*), perm-p **0.92**, noise-floor q95 **+0.033**,
> baseline AP 0.629 → +loading 0.601. 554 pairs, 10 stations, 8 years. **NULL.**

**Lane B — in-situ nutrient + NOAA replication** (`research/hab/erie_insitu_nutrient_gate.py`,
claude-f). Contemporaneous lake nutrients vs microcystin, de-seasoned + within-station:
only **lake TP** keeps a partial association (rho 0.225, perm-p 2e-4) — and that is bloom
**co-product** co-variation (cells contain P), not a forecast driver. SRP, N, TP:N all wash.
Year-level NOAA spring-load→severity (n=8): directionally consistent for **chlorophyll**
(rho 0.74, p=0.04) but **not** for the toxin (microcystin-median rho 0.52, p=0.19).

**Critic — adversarial refutation** (claude-critic, independent from-raw reimplementation).
Attacked the null 5 ways: weaker/seasononly baselines (loading lift negative at every level);
WHO >8 µg/L target (−0.053); chlorophyll target (−0.030, perm-p 0.96); phycocyanin (+0.011,
perm-p 0.12 fails); the canonical NOAA spring→Aug–Sep framing (+0.015 AP but **year-perm-p
0.156**, collapses at n=8); load math + power. **NULL SURVIVES every attack.**

**Lane C — buoyancy mechanism (calm + warm)** (`research/hab/erie_strat_met_gate.py`).
The remaining in-lake lever: *Microcystis* floats into surface scum in sustained calm, warm
water. GLERL casts are surface-only (no true stratification), so we tested the proxy —
in-situ physical state (lagged) + antecedent NASA POWER met (calm-fraction, longest calm run,
warmth+anomaly, solar, precip over 3/7/14 d at the WE centroid). The automated gate initially
flagged a "pass," but **decomposition killed it as a false positive** (`erie_strat_met_decomp.json`):
the actual calm/warm **met washes** (ΔAP −0.017, perm-p 0.75); the lone passing arm (6 in-situ
features, +0.032) does not localize — optical bloom-proxies (Secchi/turbidity/DO) are null, the
physical group sits *exactly* on its noise floor (+0.023 vs floor +0.023), and **no single feature
clears its own noise floor**. It was small-n multi-feature HGBT noise inflation that luck-passed
1 of 3 arm perm-nulls — the Alexandrium +0.04-noise-floor trap again. **Buoyancy lever: NULL.**

## The honest scientific conclusion
1. **Loading sets the seasonal/annual ENVELOPE, not the sub-seasonal forecast.** Spring P load
   tracks that year's *chlorophyll* (biomass) — consistent with the published Lake Erie
   relationship — but provides **no station-level forecast skill** for microcystin occurrence
   once season + autocorrelation are controlled. Minimum detectable ΔAP at this N is ~0.02–0.03;
   every observed loading effect is ≤ 0.
2. **Toxin ≠ biomass.** Loading relates to biomass (chl rho 0.74) far better than to the toxin
   (mc rho 0.52, n.s.). Microcystin is governed by strain/regulation dynamics loading can't see.
3. **The cyano driver-null now generalizes to the hardest possible test** — real toxin labels +
   gold-standard tributary loading in the most nutrient-driven freshwater HAB on Earth. This
   closes the two levers [[algae-bloom-frontier]] left open. It is a definitive **negative**,
   not a manufactured breakthrough.

## National generalization (the properly-powered test)
Erie is one system (n=554 pairs, 8 years) — and Lane C showed n=554 has a +0.03 AP noise
floor that can fake a "pass." So we built the **first national in-situ microcystin toxin
corpus** to test the driver-null with real power:

- `data/external_raw/wqp_microcystin/` — 68,924 WQP grab-sample microcystin measurements,
  5,905 sites, 2008–2024 (`ops/data_fetch/fetch_wqp_microcystin.py`). Censoring handled
  (non-detects "<0.13"/"ND"/"U" → below-threshold, not dropped — they're 18% of data).
- `data/external_curated/wqp_microcystin_panel/panel.parquet` — 32,812-visit panel, 3,564
  sites, **49 states** (`research/hab/build_national_microcystin_panel.py`).
- `data/external_raw/wqp_microcystin_met/` — per-site NASA POWER daily met, 432 well-sampled
  sites (`ops/data_fetch/fetch_nasapower_microcystin_sites.py`).

**National forecastability** (new result): in-situ **toxin** blooms forecast at pooled LOYO AP
**0.774** (vs persistence ~0.56) from history+season+site alone — the CyAN *satellite* finding
([[algae-bloom-frontier]]) now confirmed on the real toxin, 49 states. NOTE (critic-2 caveat):
the pooled AP is optimistic vs the **macro within-site AP ~0.57** (mean within-site base rate
0.285) — cross-site ranking inflates the pooled number; the honest per-lake forecast skill is
the within-site lift (+0.28, 96.6% of sites beat their own base rate). CENSORING FIX (critic-2):
the panel builder originally dropped ~13.8k non-detects with a blank result-unit; recovering
them via the detection-limit fields lowered the base rate 0.194→0.141 and the headline ~0.80→0.78.

**National driver test** (`research/hab/national_microcystin_driver_gate.py`): adding 18
antecedent met features (calm-fraction, warmth+anomaly, solar, precip, wind over 7/14/30 d)
to that baseline, evaluated under BOTH leave-year-out and grouped leave-site-out:

> Result (corrected censoring-fixed panel): ΔAP **−0.007** (leave-year-out) and **−0.0024**
> (leave-site-out) — both below the +0.005 gate. **Noise floor is NEGATIVE** at n~21k — random
> features cannot manufacture lift here, unlike Erie's n=554. **NULL, robustly.** Met hurts even
> vs a season-only baseline; met-alone AP (0.335) ≈ the base rate. Zero forecast skill nationally.

This is the decisive generalization: the cyano driver-null holds for the real toxin, at
national scale (431 lakes / 49 states), against meteorology — and the negative noise floor
proves the result is power-real, not an artifact. It also explains Erie's Lane C false
positive (small-n inflated floor).

## What WOULD still be worth trying (kept, not done)
- **In-lake stratification / water-column temperature** at the station (vertical structure
  drives *Microcystis* buoyancy) — a different mechanism than upstream loading.
- **Strain / qPCR mcyE toxin-gene** labels — the toxin-regulation lever, genuinely unseen here.
- n is the wall on the seasonal relationship: more bloom-years (recover 2020–2022 station ids;
  add other GLERL/IFCB basins) would let the spring-load→severity relation be properly null-tested.

## Reproduce
```
python research/hab/erie_microcystin_driver_gate.py     # Lane A  -> erie_microcystin_driver_gate.json
python research/hab/erie_insitu_nutrient_gate.py        # Lane B  -> erie_insitu_nutrient_gate.json
# critic probes: reports/hab/erie/_critic_probe.py, _critic_springgate.py
```
Coordination + ledger + learnings: `reports/hab/erie/{COORDINATION,erie_intents.json,erie_learnings.jsonl}`.

reports/tokyo_bay_tbeic/lakehouse_shadow.baseline_policy: Compare each feature family against station-month and persistence-now; mark positives only with station-block bootstrap support.

leakage_report.label_rule: Targets are station-wise future labels created by shift(-horizon); label date is origin_date + horizon.
leakage_report.feature_rule: Feature sets use station/month, Tokyo lag/rolling physical drivers, shadow lag/rolling all-raw aggregates, and l2_openmeteo lag/rolling columns only.

_meta.question: How many days ahead can we predict FIB beach exceedance with genuine skill OVER STATION MEMORY (a multi-feature memory-only HGBT), honestly? (Not over single-bit persistence — that is the weak baseline a critic caught the prior version overclaiming on.)
_meta.headline_baseline: STATION-MEMORY MODEL: HGBT with the same learner/CV/folds as the main model, trained on ONLY the memory/lab-history features (MEMORY_FEATS: exc_prev/exc_prev2/exc_roll5_prev/exc_roll10_prev/station_prior_rate/station_prior_n/days_since_prev/ent|fec|tot|ecoli_prev+_roll3_prev) — NO rain, NO environment, NO season-beyond-memory, NO geography/context.
_meta.win_criterion: HEADLINE: lead L>=1 where the full model beats the STATION-MEMORY MODEL by a CI-separated paired-AP margin (bootstrap 95% CI excludes 0) — i.e. the environmental/forecast features add real skill over the autocorrelation backbone. The single-bit persistence-at-L + climatology comparison is RETAINED but explicitly labelled the WEAK baseline; a beat over it does NOT define the horizon.
_meta.overclaim_fix: A critic caught the prior version claiming a 3-day horizon by comparing only against single-bit persistence (AP ~0.18-0.20). The honest horizon OVER the station-memory MODEL is ~1 day: real CI-separated win at L=0 (+0.16 AP) and L=1 (+0.08 AP), a TIE at L=2 (~+0.01, CI may touch 0), and a LOSS at L=3 (~-0.03). Beyond ~1 day the full model is just station memory + noise.
_meta.leakage_discipline: lab/memory features use reveal_lag_days=L (predecessor sample date <= t-L); rainfall windows END at t-L (lagged rain, no numerical weather forecast available locally -> antecedent-rain proxy is a CONSERVATIVE lower bound, never leaks; a real precip forecast could only do better). climatology fit on train only. NO same-day or future leakage.
_meta.label: enterococcus
caveats: No local numerical weather forecast: forecast uses LAGGED/antecedent rain (windows end at t-L). This is the honest no-forecast lower bound; a skillful precip forecast at 1-3d could recover some of the lost rain signal.

Under ~weekly beach sampling the lab predecessor is typically already >=7d stale, so persistence-at-L barely changes with L -> the BAR (persistence) is nearly as strong at L=3 as at L=0. Beating it is what would prove forecast skill.
EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO is the honest headline; ALL is reported but inflated by the San Diego/Tijuana 2022 regime break.
Margins use the RAW-ranking model AP (isotonic is monotone -> same ranking). Calibrated AP is reported alongside for deploy-readiness.

forecast_horizon.verdict: HONEST: forecast skill OVER STATION MEMORY = 2 day(s). Environmental/forecast features add CI-separated skill at L=0 and out to lead 2d over the multi-feature station-memory MODEL; beyond that the full model is just station memory + noise (TIE/LOSS). Per-lead margin over the station-memory MODEL: L=0 +0.159 [0.1487,0.1683] WIN; L=1 +0.076 [0.0676,0.084] WIN; L=2 +0.016 [0.0081,0.0233] WIN; L=3 -0.018 [-0.025,-0.0103] LOSS. reports/estate_search/FINDINGS.md.text:
# Estate-wide GPU Signal Search — FINDINGS

**Date:** 2026-06-19/20 · **Compute:** RTX 5090 (torch 2.11.0+cu128, CUDA) · **Orchestration:** `estate-gpu-signal-search` workflow (8 agents: recon + 3×build + 3×adversarial-critic + synthesis)

## Mandate
Jon: "run every possible permutation of all signals" / "use the GPU harder" / "all value no bloat" / "we want all the data even if it doesn't find anything, as long as it's real and accurate." Reframe: we had only ever tested **linear subsets of ~20 hand-picked signals**. This searched the **nonlinear functional-form + interaction space over ~1,000+ pooled estate columns**, with valid multiple-testing control, on the GPU.

## Bottom line
**Clean, validly-searched DRIVER-NULL across the entire functional-form + interaction space.** Zero genuinely new signals survive FDR **and** beat the frozen bacteria baseline (calibrated AP **0.5099**, EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO). Everything high-scoring is rainfall, station-memory, geography/IDs, or self-referential artifacts. The null — at this scale — **is the deliverable.** Nothing ships; headline unchanged.

## Engines (all RECORD = honest null)

### 1. GPU exhaustive interaction scan (epistasis-style) — `interaction_scan_gpu.py`
- **474,825** pairwise interactions over 975 train-usable estate cols, **4.3M pairs/s**, partial-corr-of-(z_i·z_j)-with-additive-residual statistic.
- Control: BH-FDR (q=0.10) → 828 pass; **300-perm max-stat FWER (p<0.05) → 2 pass**; both gated through a per-row HGBT A/B (interaction-over-main-effects + leave-one-beach-out).
- **0 real survivors.** The one temporally-promising pair (Tokyo Bay surface-salinity × its own lag1, ΔAP +0.0117) is per-station memorization → **LOBO −0.0002**, and is two lags of the same Tokyo variable geographically irrelevant to CA beaches.
- Critic: REAL, leakage-controlled, reproduced; synthetic test recovered a planted interaction (stat 0.957) and rejected a pure main effect (~0.04). **Verdict: RECORD.**

### 2. GPU symbolic regression (auto-discover π-groups) — `symbolic_reg_run.py`
- **3,000,000** expression trees (depth≤3, 12-op set) @ ~7k formulas/s, scored vs the additive FEATS+rain **residual**.
- Best in-sample skill 0.0275 **beats** the permutation null (max 0.0121, p=0.032) → real in-sample structure. But **4 of 5 Pareto formulas just rediscover rainfall** (`nasa_power::PRECTOTCORR`).
- The lone OOT/BH-FDR "survivor" (`ratio(exp(roms::v), sqrt(cdip::airTemp))`): **WASH at signal_lab** (ΔAP +0.0014 < 0.005) **and statistically invalid** — its inputs have 0–1 test-era days, so the feature is a near-constant train-mean fill.
- Critic: REAL GPU run, correct bottom line. **Must-fix (do not cite the "survivor"):** ran over a shared cache matrix with residual FIB leakage; enforce per-column train≥15 ∧ test≥8 day coverage + effective-N guard before Fisher-z; permutation-null framing is anti-conservative not conservative. **Verdict: RECORD.**

### 3. Deep knockoffs (DeepLINK/DeepPINK) — `deep_knockoffs_select.py`
- Competitive-input DNN over original+knockoff features, **knockoff+ FDR** (Candès 2018) q=0.10, 5 seeds, null-symmetric W.
- **0 of 321 candidates** (24 frozen FEATS + 297 estate) survive — τ=∞ all seeds, still 0 at q=0.50.
- **Positive control passes:** `station_prior_rate` ranks #1 by importance (correct rediscovery of the known PRIMARY) — but even it can't clear the finite-sample bound → the null is honest but **partly uninformative/underpowered** at our n/p. Reproduces the lab-memory linear model-X result.
- Critic: REAL, valid FDR (not raw top-k), reproduced. **Must-fix:** wide matrix leaked FIB target-family (`ca_safe_to_swim_fib::geomean_*`) + geo/id/depth columns into the candidate set; null unaffected (a leak only manufactures false positives) but the "leakage-free" wording is overstated; report the statistically-preferred `daily` mode. **Verdict: RECORD.**

## Leakage caveat (does NOT change the nulls)
Two of three wide matrices had incomplete exclude filters — FIB target-family columns (`ca_safe_to_swim_fib::result/geomean_30day/geomean_6week`, `ceden_water_chem::Enterococcus`, `wqp::Enterococcus`, `echo_dmr::Enterococci`) and geo/id/depth/row_number columns reached the candidate set. **A leak can only manufacture a false positive; none occurred, so every null stands.** Must be fixed before any future *positive* from these matrices is trusted.

## What survived nothing tells us
The consistent verdict across interaction-space, formula-space, and knockoff-space: **the lever is not a richer functional form over the existing estate.** It is:
1. **A low-autocorrelation target** — bacteria dry-onset slice, `hypoxia_lead`, cyano onset/anomaly — where the driver-null is known to be escapable (vs the near-ceiling pooled daily bacteria series).
2. **A new modality / objective** — molecular SD ddPCR labels, credentialed FES2014/ERA5/CMEMS physics, or an objective change (triage / transfer / lead-time).

## Next actions
1. **DO NOT promote anything.** Headline stays 0.5099. All three = RECORD.
2. **Catalog:** log the three searched-and-null spaces under `bacteria_exceedance` (done — `estate_interaction_2way`, `estate_symbolic_formula`, `estate_deep_knockoffs`).
3. **Leakage correctness-fix** (before any future positive from these matrices): add `ca_safe_to_swim_fib` + `ca_safe_to_swim_fib_excluded` to the gauntlet `EXCLUDE_PATH_RE`, add `result|geomean|Enterococc|coliform` to `EXCLUDE_COL_RE`, and drop geo/id/depth/pressure/row_number columns.
4. **Scale the valid search** to low-autocorrelation targets — the engines are clean and reusable; point them at the dry-onset / hypoxia / cyano-onset targets.
5. **Data, not form:** prioritize the blocked credentialed fetches (FES2014/ERA5/CMEMS) and molecular labels over more in-estate search.

## Reproduce
```
# interaction scan
python research/estate_search/interaction_scan_matrix.py --max-rows-per-table 120000
python research/estate_search/interaction_scan_gpu.py --target bacteria_exceedance --n-perm 300 --top-ab 8
# symbolic regression
python -m research.estate_search.symbolic_reg_matrix --rebuild
python -m research.estate_search.symbolic_reg_run --n-formulas 3000000 --batch 8000 --n-perm 30 --seed 42
# deep knockoffs
python research/estate_search/deep_knockoffs_wide_matrix.py
python research/estate_search/deep_knockoffs_select.py --q 0.1 --seeds 5 --epochs 150 --mode daily
```

red_team_robustness.concern_selection_on_driver: wet_tail is defined by rain_3d>=p90, which could handicap the rain-blind free model on within-stratum rain gradient. verdict_vocabulary.PRIMARY: This signal IS (the core of) the validated model for that target.

HARDENED 2026-06-19 (eval_standard ClaimCard, reports/_eval_cards/hypoxia_card.json): survives a permutation null (p=0.005) AND BH-FDR across all 5 horizons (h7 q=0.0083, h14 q=0.0083 SURVIVE; 21d +0.006 and 28d +0.003 correctly WASH) AND a station-clustered bootstrap CI [0.011, 0.053]; decision-curve valuable across the whole cost grid; strongest in the currently-oxic-origin onset slice (+0.029 = genuine lead-skill). Scope is 7-14d (not 7d+). Carrier = thermal cap + warmth, NOT haline/wind/chl; deep nets (Transformer/GRU) do not beat the tree

target_headlines.headline: ONSET reframe WEAKENS the hypoxia_lead escape: STRAT lift is CI-separated ONLY at h=3d (+0.0545 AP, station-block CI [0.014,0.084]) and washes (CI includes 0) at every multi-week lead 7-28d in the TEMPORAL holdout. The two holdouts DISAGREE — leave-station-out shows ~0 lift at h=3 but +0.028 at h=7 — so NO single horizon survives BOTH holdouts CI-separated. Verdict: WASH at deployable multi-week lead. The model still beats the better naive at every h (seasonality + DO-proximity), but the *stratification driver* adds no robust onset lead-skill beyond persist+season. The parent state-target '+0.02 @7-14d' does not transfer to the harder onset decision.

project_docs.title: Monterey Bay AI Lab
project_docs.text:
# Monterey Bay AI Lab

**A multi-hazard coastal warning lab for rare water-quality events — anchored on two decades of Monterey Bay mooring physics.**

The Monterey Bay AI Lab is a **multi-hazard coastal warning lab**. Its two current claimable pillars are **statewide bacterial-exceedance warning** and **domoic-acid / HAB next-visit warning**; both are event-forecasting problems where the model beats an honest operational baseline. The 21-year MBARI M1/M2 mooring record is the physical **backbone** that grounds and calibrates the work, not the forecasting headline.

project_docs.title: Monterey Bay AI Lab
project_docs.text: ## Core Capabilities

project_docs.title: Monterey Bay AI Lab
project_docs.text:
### 1. Statewide Event-Driven Forecasting — the headline
A multimodal engine predicting rare, non-persistent events (bacterial exceedances) across 800+ California stations.
- **Verified margin:** on a statewide California enterococcus benchmark (1.32M lab **assays** → ~442k modeled station-days, of which ~89k are in the deployable-stratum test split; test events ≥2022), a calibrated gradient-boosted model reaches **ROC-AUC 0.868 / AP 0.5099** on the **statewide deployable stratum** (California marine enterococcus in the standard rainfall/runoff regime) — the honest headline, since the cross-border San Diego/Tijuana plume is a 2022 data-regime artifact and is reported separately, not pooled. It beats the deployed AB411 rain rule (AP 0.18) and a Virtual-Beach-class MLR (AP 0.38), is well-calibrated after isotonic calibration (ECE 0.009 at the operational 2-day lab-result reveal lag — the AP/ECE include the confirmed harmonic spring-neap tidal-range escape, +0.011 AP over rain+discharge; see `signals/catalog.yaml` `tide_range_springneap` — the tracked, byte-reproducible `expected/` outputs; see `MODEL_SUITE.md`), and generalizes spatially: it beats both the regulatory rule and station-memory in **9/9 leave-one-county-out folds**. Decision-support-grade. (The older "AUC 0.888" headline was the artifact-inflated pooled/multi-analyte figure and has been retired — see `research/bacteria/reproduce/PAPER_DRAFT.md`, which derives the honest deployable stratum and the reveal-lag-enforced metrics above.)
- **The honest frontier:** site memory is a strong baseline (a recently-dirty beach tends to stay dirty; station-memory alone reaches AUC ~0.77 / AP 0.28 on the same statewide deployable stratum). The model already beats it meaningfully (+0.23 AP) with environmental drivers, and — verified — **generalizes to beaches it was never trained on**: under leave-one-beach-out cross-validation the driver model holds **AP 0.494 / ROC-AUC 0.852** on never-seen beaches, still beating station-memory (AP 0.28) and the AB411 rule (AP 0.18), so the skill is a transferable environmental pattern, not per-station memorization. The open frontier is extending risk estimates to the beaches we **don't** monitor. A learned spatial surface from *raw* station coordinates or a nearest-neighbor exceedance lag turns out to be a **wash** — no measurable lift (all spatial arms AP 0.497) because the per-station / county / statewide rate features already encode location — while residual per-beach autocorrelation remains (Moran's I ≈ 0.20, one-sided p<0.01). The obvious next lever — **physical spatial covariates** (outfall / river-mouth distance, embayment) — was then tested and is *also* a wash (it underperforms the lat/lon arm by −0.028 AP under leave-one-county-out and does not generalize), so there is currently **no demonstrated lever** that turns the residual clustering into transferable skill; it remains an open problem. These results reproduce from `research/bacteria/spatial_autocorr.py` (leave-one-beach-out + Moran's I), `research/bacteria/spatial_drivers_experiment.py` (the raw-coordinate wash), and `research/bacteria/physical_spatial_covariates.py` (the physical-covariate wash).

## How the pipeline reads data

Code never hardcodes a path or cloud project. Configure via environment variables:

- `MBAL_PROJECT_ROOT` — repo / working root
- `MBAL_SOURCE_PARQUET` — curated source panel
- `MBAL_CACHE_DIR` — feature/driver cache (`nn_cache`)
- `MBAL_LAKEHOUSE_DIR` — lakehouse outputs (`lakehouse`)
- `MBAL_GCP_PROJECT` — your own GCP project, if pulling from BigQuery

project_docs.title: Model Suite - what exists, what has deps, what is claimable
project_docs.text:
## Legend
- **neural?** - `neural`/`foundation`/`continual` families are neural; `tree`/`baseline` are not. (The `continual` family is reserved and currently has no registered model: the dense mixture-of-experts design once kept here was retired as bloat, and what survives is a compact 2,772,504-param Conv1d+MLP forecaster in `sota_continual_learning/`, not registered as a claimable model.)
- **deps available?** - whether the model's declared dependencies import in this environment. This is not an execution smoke test for the registry entrypoint.
- **beats baseline?** - against the honest baseline (best-naive for forecasting; AB411 / Virtual-Beach MLR / station-memory for bacteria), stated straight.
- **claimable?** - safe to put in a pitch/paper. Only `production_candidate` (and, case-by-case, a promoted `benchmark`) with on-disk evidence qualifies.

## Decision

Status: **local production gate passed; senior-ready gate is held on true external-smoke evidence and current high-risk worktree state.**

The project is now shaped as a production candidate: source control is initialized,
generated artifacts are ignored, unit tests pass, release-gate tooling exists, and the
portable CPU smoke job succeeded from `C:\mbal_portable_smoke`. It is not approved for
external deployment, global SOTA claims, or broad driver-value claims. Promotion remains

runner writes `release_gate/reports/external_smoke_report.json` with runner-owned storage

project_docs.title: Monterey Bay AI Lab Production Lakehouse Contracts
project_docs.text:
# Monterey Bay AI Lab Production Lakehouse Contracts

Generated: 2026-06-07

This project uses a production-grade local lakehouse architecture. Neural runs emit
structured artifacts under `lakehouse/` ensuring the workflow remains portable to
generic remote compute environments (Spark, Kubernetes, object storage, or managed
runtime platforms) without changing the core modeling contract.

project_docs.title: Monterey Bay AI Lab Production Lakehouse Contracts
project_docs.text:
## Layers

- `lakehouse/silver/forecast_splits/`
  Canonical split contracts. Each `split_id` records the expanding-window cutoffs,
  train window bounds, evaluated horizons, source fingerprint, and run configuration.

- `lakehouse/gold/forecast_runs/`
  Run manifests. Each `run_id` records model, loss, driver configuration, source
  fingerprint, cache version, environment knobs, and artifact paths.

- `lakehouse/gold/forecast_metrics/`
  Normalized target/horizon metrics keyed by `run_id`, `split_id`, `unique_id`,
  and `horizon_h`. Carries the honest-baseline columns `seasonal_naive_rmse` and
  `skill_vs_best_naive_pct` (skill vs the better of persistence and seasonal-naive),
  backfilled by `ops/backfill_seasonal_naive.py` from the prediction partitions.

- `lakehouse/gold/forecast_predictions/`
  Per-run prediction partitions keyed by `run_id`. These preserve the original
  `cv_predictions.parquet` shape while adding `run_id`, `split_id`, and prediction
  column metadata.

project_docs.title: Monterey Bay AI Lab Production Lakehouse Contracts
project_docs.text:
## Architectural Tier Mapping

- **Bronze (Raw/Ingest):**
  Immutable copies of raw MBARI source data and external driver inputs.

- **Silver (Curated/Enriched):**
  QC-applied hourly matrix, observed mask, driver availability table, and
  `forecast_splits`. These are ready for modeling and feature engineering.

- **Gold (Aggregated/Insights):**
  `forecast_runs`, `forecast_metrics`, `forecast_predictions`, and final comparison
  leaderboards. These are consumer-ready artifacts for scientists and managers.

project_docs.text:
# MBAI Remote Research & Data Access Contract

This document defines how the Monterey Bay AI Lab (MBAI) handles scaling normalized signals while protecting local compute resources and managing Google Cloud costs.

project_docs.title: MBAI Remote Research & Data Access Contract
project_docs.text:
## 1. The "Forge" vs. The "Evidence Layer"

We operate on a **Push-Only Architecture**.

project_docs.title: MBAI Remote Research & Data Access Contract
project_docs.text:
### The MBAI Forge (User's Machine / RTX 5090)
- **Role:** High-Performance Training (HPT), data normalization, and heuristic fusion.
- **Security:** Private and isolated. **No inbound traffic allowed.**
- **Workflow:** 
  1. Ingest raw data from Google BigQuery (one-time or scheduled sync).
  2. Perform multivariate regression and A/B testing locally.
  3. Emit normalized **Gold Artifacts** (Parquet files).
  4. **Push** Gold Artifacts to the Google Cloud Evidence Layer.

project_docs.title: Value Gate — is this worth building, or is it bloat?
project_docs.text:
# Value Gate — is this worth building, or is it bloat?

Binding for all agents (Codex, Claude, Gemini, Qwen). Run this gate **before you
suggest OR execute** any new feature, module, abstraction, driver, data source, model,
metric, doc, or "phase." Its job: keep the project continually improving **without**
accreting bloat. Cite the result with a **falsifiable payload** — a bare "beats baseline"
is itself a gate failure (it's how cargo-cult citation gets caught):
*"Value gate: DO_NOW — beats <baseline> by <Δmetric>, test <path::name>"* or
*"rejected: <kill-category> — <one-line reason>"*.

This gate is itself held to its own bar: it is one short checklist, not a manifesto.

project_docs.title: Value Gate — is this worth building, or is it bloat?
project_docs.text:
## The 30-second gate — all five must be YES to build

1. **Beats a real baseline / answers a real question.** Does it measurably improve a
   result against an *honest* baseline (seasonal-naive, persistence, AB411, prior-lab,
   station-memory, the current metric) — or answer a question a stakeholder actually
   asked? "It's more sophisticated / more SOTA / more general" is **not** a yes.
2. **Additive & reversible — OR a correctness fix.** New *behavior* lands behind a
   default-off flag or as a new file and is removable cleanly (Delta phases, shadow
   dual-write off-by-default: yes; an in-place rewrite of the gold layer for a new
   feature: no). **Correctness fixes** (leakage, dedup key, median-not-mean, causality)
   are EXEMPT — they may be in-place and one-way, and fixing them is mandatory
   regardless of reversibility (see the data-correctness invariants in `AGENTS.md`).
3. **Testable, and you will test it.** Is there a concrete pass/fail test or a
   reproducible number you will add in the same change? If you can't test it, you can't
   claim it.
4. **Valuable NOW, not speculative.** Its value must be demonstrable now, not unprovable
   until some future step lands. Sequencing is fine — building a holdout harness *before*
   the validation it enables passes; building a feature whose worth can't be shown until
   a burn-in/dataset/evidence step that hasn't happened does not. When in doubt, do the
   cheaper enabling step first.
5. **Genuinely new, and improves a result we actually claim.** Not redundant with an
   existing path, and not *polishing* a case we deliberately **exclude** (e.g. tuning San
   Diego's calibration) or a stratum we don't deploy. **Carve-out:** work that would
   *change* the exclusion decision — expand coverage, or fix the reason a region was
   excluded — is a baseline-beating feature and PASSES.

If any answer is NO → **do not build it.** State which kill-category it is.

project_docs.title: Value Gate — is this worth building, or is it bloat?
project_docs.text:
## Kill list — name the reason when you reject something

- **Bloat** — needless complexity/abstraction; recovers what a simpler thing already gives.
- **Vapor** — scaffolding not wired to real work; a "framework" with no caller.
- **Premature** — depends on a burn-in / evidence / data step not yet done.
- **Ops-toggle** — a config/env flip or one-line invocation, not an engineering deliverable.
- **Redundant** — an existing path already covers it.
- **Cosmetic** — renames, reformatting, micro-metrics that change no decision.
- **Excluded-case polish** — optimizes a region/stratum/path we deliberately set aside
  *without changing the exclusion decision* (tuning excluded San Diego's calibration).
  NB: work that makes an excluded case *includable* is NOT this — it passes (see Q5).

# Open-Source Readiness

This checklist defines the minimum bar before publishing the repository publicly.

project_docs.title: Open-Source Readiness
project_docs.text:
## Required

- `README.md` explains the project, setup, tests, Docker workflow, and data boundaries.
- `LICENSE` grants explicit open-source rights.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` explains branch, test, and pull request expectations.
- `SECURITY.md` gives a private vulnerability reporting path.
- `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` sets collaboration expectations.
- `pyproject.toml` declares package metadata and dependency groups.
- `Dockerfile` and `.dockerignore` support clean reproducibility checks.
- Tests pass with `python ops/run_tests.py`.

project_docs.title: Open-Source Readiness
project_docs.text:
## Data Boundary

Do not publish private credentials, local `.env` files, raw protected datasets, generated caches, or workstation-only artifacts. Public releases should include one of the following:

- Small synthetic fixtures.
- Publicly licensed sample data.
- Scripts that download public data from documented sources.
- Precomputed public artifacts with provenance and version notes.

# MBAL Public-Repo Publish Runbook

**Status:** prep-complete; the destructive history purge is **NOT** run by this runbook
automatically — it is gated behind explicit owner approval (see Step 4).

This runbook is the **executable, fail-closed** sequence for cutting the public open-source
release of the Monterey Bay AI Lab repo. It exists because the working repo carries things a
public repo must never have:

- ~3.9 GB of raw third-party data (the 50 `data/external_raw/dpr_pur/pur19xx.zip` PUR zips,
  16 of them >100 MB — these also block `git push` to GitHub entirely),
- committed `*.xlsx` workbooks and other large/binary blobs in **history**,
- a personal contact email and private GCP identifiers (project `monterey-bay-lab`, the two
  GCS buckets, the BigQuery dataset) hardcoded in source,
- third-party collaborator PII under `docs/collab/` (untracked today, but must be kept out).

The non-negotiable rule: **nothing leaves this tree until `ops/prepublish_gate.py` prints
`RESULT: PASS`.** The gate is fail-closed (any blocker → exit 1).

project_docs.title: BEATING DEPLOYED PRACTICE IN BEACH-WATER QUALITY NOWCASTING: A LEAKAGE-CLEAN, CALIBRATED, STATEWIDE BENCHMARK ON THE CAL
project_docs.text:
## ABSTRACT

Beach advisories for fecal-indicator-bacteria (FIB) exceedance are issued from laboratory
cultures that return roughly 18-24 hours after a water sample is collected, so the same-day
public-health decision is effectively unguided or relies on a coarse wet-weather rule
(California AB411). We build a statewide California benchmark (1.32M assays, 802 monitoring
stations, 17 counties, 2005-2026) for NOWCASTING single-sample marine enterococcus
exceedance (>104 MPN/100 mL) using only information available at sampling time. With strictly
causal features — prior laboratory results enforced against the true lab-reveal lag, station
memory, seasonality, gridded rainfall, and USGS river first-flush discharge — a gradient-
boosted decision-tree model (with a harmonic spring-neap tidal-range feature), isotonically
calibrated, attains Average Precision (AP) 0.510 /
AUROC 0.868 on the honest statewide stratum. It beats, on the same data: the AB411 rain rule
(AP 0.178; ~2.8x), a Virtual-Beach-class per-site multiple logistic regression representing
DEPLOYED practice (AP 0.375; +0.12 AP, ~32%), and a station-memory baseline (AP 0.278) —
while remaining deploy-ready in calibration (Expected Calibration Error, ECE, 0.020). Under
leave-one-county-out (LOCO) spatial cross-validation, the model beats AB411 and station
memory in 9/9 held-out counties. We report negative and null results honestly: a 2022
San Diego / Tijuana-River sewage REGIME BREAK inflates pooled metrics and is excluded from
the headline; river discharge is largely rain-redundant; and a static probability calibrator
degrades under regime shift, which a prequential (online) recalibrator only partially repairs.
We release a leakage-controlled, reproducible benchmark and argue that the field's
publishable contribution is not a higher pooled AUC but a calibrated model that beats deployed
practice under honest, stratified, spatially-held-out evaluation.

---

project_docs.title: BEATING DEPLOYED PRACTICE IN BEACH-WATER QUALITY NOWCASTING: A LEAKAGE-CLEAN, CALIBRATED, STATEWIDE BENCHMARK ON THE CAL
project_docs.text:
## 1. INTRODUCTION

Recreational coastal waters are monitored for fecal-indicator bacteria; when a single-sample
standard is exceeded, an advisory or closure is posted. The operational bottleneck is time:
the culture-based laboratory assay returns ~18-24 hours after sampling, so on the day a
beachgoer is exposed, the manager either has yesterday's result or, under California's AB411
program, a wet-weather rule that posts an advisory when rainfall exceeds a threshold. AB411 is
known to be weak; the predictive tool actually deployed at many modern beach programs is EPA
Virtual Beach, a per-site multiple-linear/logistic regression on local hydrometeorological
drivers, or a USGS site-specific nowcast.

A large body of work reports machine-learning models for FIB nowcasting, often with
impressive pooled AUC. Two failure modes recur and motivate this paper. First, LEAKAGE: many
"prior result" features are constructed by sample index (the previous SAMPLE), not by reveal
time (the previous RESULT that had physically returned by prediction time); and spatially
pooled features can leak a held-out region into training. Second, ARTIFACTS: a model evaluated
across a measurement/program discontinuity can score the discontinuity rather than generalizable
skill, inflating a headline number that does not survive domain scrutiny.

Our contribution is an honest benchmark. We (i) define a single, standard-aligned target — the
AB411 marine enterococcus single-sample standard; (ii) construct strictly causal features with
an enforced lab-reveal lag; (iii) evaluate against a baseline ladder that includes a
Virtual-Beach-class model representing deployed practice, not only the weak AB411 rule;
(iv) stratify by region and era to surface, rather than hide, a regime break; (v) test spatial
generalization by leave-one-county-out; and (vi) treat calibration as an explicit deploy gate.
We find a model that beats deployed practice and generalizes — and we report where it does not.

---

project_docs.title: 10 Meaningful Predictive Cross-Dataset Findings (Critic-Revised & Purified)
project_docs.text:
# 10 Meaningful Predictive Cross-Dataset Findings (Critic-Revised & Purified)

Following a rigorous red-team critique, we struck down all findings vulnerable to shared-decadal climate trends, effort confounds (e.g., beach sampling mandates), or sensor spatial smearing. The following 10 findings have survived strict detrending (year-over-year differences) and localized spatial-pairing, proving robust physical coupling across completely independent data sources.

project_docs.title: 10 Meaningful Predictive Cross-Dataset Findings (Critic-Revised & Purified)
project_docs.text:
## 🌍 Climate $\rightarrow$ Ocean Physics
**1. NPGO $\rightarrow$ Coastal Upwelling (A4)**
*Datasets:* `npgo` $\times$ `upwelling_beuti`
*Finding:* The North Pacific Gyre Oscillation strongly predicts coastal upwelling at latitude 37N ($\rho=+0.161$) over a 38-year record.

**2. ENSO / PDO $\rightarrow$ Sea Surface Temperature (A5)**
*Datasets:* `global_ocean_indices` $\times$ `ersst_monthly`
*Finding:* El Niño (ONI) and PDO predict central CA offshore sea surface temperatures. ONI $\rho=+0.488$, PDO $\rho=+0.450$.

project_docs.title: 10 Meaningful Predictive Cross-Dataset Findings (Critic-Revised & Purified)
project_docs.text:
## 🌊 Coastal Physics $\rightarrow$ Biology & Thermodynamics
**3. Coastal Wind $\rightarrow$ Sea Surface Temperature (A2)**
*Datasets:* `ndbc_stdmet` $\times$ `mur_sst`
*Finding:* Strong alongshore winds lead to coastal cooling via upwelling ($\rho=-0.04$, best lag 1 day).

**4. Upwelling $\rightarrow$ Phytoplankton Blooms (A3)**
*Datasets:* `upwelling_beuti` $\times$ `viirs_chl`
*Finding:* Nutrient-rich deep water upwelling predicts offshore chlorophyll-a with a ~5 day lead time ($\rho=0.158$).

**5. Upwelling $\rightarrow$ Localized Coastal Cooling (F24)**
*Datasets:* `upwelling_indices` $\times$ `cdip_sst_network`
*Finding:* By spatially pairing Upwelling Index latitudes to the strictly *nearest* coastal CDIP buoy, localized upwelling strongly predicts daily local temperature anomalies ($\rho=-0.120$, $n=83,629$). 

**6. Upwelling $\rightarrow$ Deep Mooring Cooling (F28)**
*Datasets:* `upwelling_indices` $\times$ `cencoos_mbal_moorings`
*Finding:* Monterey Bay upwelling (36N) drives cooling specifically at the CENCOOS M1 offshore mooring ($\rho=-0.318$).

project_docs.title: Finding: the culture↔ddPCR enterococcus divergence is a *miscalibrated threshold* + a *structured, predictable* blind sp
project_docs.text:
# Finding: the culture↔ddPCR enterococcus divergence is a *miscalibrated threshold* + a *structured, predictable* blind spot — not noise

**Date:** 2026-06-20 · **Status:** diagnostic, changes no frozen headline · **Code:** `research/bacteria/ddpcr_divergence_structure.py` · **Data:** `reports/bacteria/ddpcr_divergence_structure.json`

project_docs.title: Finding: the culture↔ddPCR enterococcus divergence is a *miscalibrated threshold* + a *structured, predictable* blind sp
project_docs.text:
## Background
`ddpcr_label_noise.py` established (descriptively) that on 909 paired San-Diego station-days,
culture and ddPCR enterococcus disagree on the exceedance call **48%** of the time (κ=0.19),
heavily asymmetric (ddPCR-positive / culture-negative: 434 of 439 disagreements). It correctly
concluded "ddPCR is a *different* target, not a denoised culture label" and stopped there. That
left the decision-relevant question open: **is the disagreement just a bad cutoff and/or noise,
or is it structured?** This study answers both, for free, on the existing paired panel (no
external joins → no WQP/Argo data landmines).

project_docs.title: Finding: the culture↔ddPCR enterococcus divergence is a *miscalibrated threshold* + a *structured, predictable* blind sp
project_docs.text:
## Q1 — The 4× asymmetry is mostly a **miscalibrated official threshold**
- Threshold-free, the continuous ddPCR value **ranks the culture exceedance call at AUC 0.903** —
  the two assays measure largely the same latent signal.
- At the official CDPH equivalence cutoff (1413 copies/100 mL = 104 CFU), ddPCR flags exceedance
  **64.8%** of days vs culture's **17.6%** → κ=0.19 (poor).
- The base-rate-matched ddPCR cutoff is **~65,400 copies/100 mL (~46× higher)**; at that cutoff
  agreement jumps to **κ=0.59 (moderate)**.
- **Read:** the official ddPCR→culture equivalence threshold appears set far too low on this panel,
  manufacturing most of the "4× over-flagging." This is a concrete, testable recalibration claim.

project_docs.title: ERROR ATLAS — Deployable Bacteria Nowcasting Baseline
project_docs.text:
# ERROR ATLAS — Deployable Bacteria Nowcasting Baseline

**Mission:** REDISCOVER THE OCEAN (Lane B). **Owner/writer:** claude-ocean-laneB (single-writer file).
**Subject:** HGBT + isotonic, FEATS + rain + discharge + site-memory, EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO stratum.
**Reproduction gate (PASSED, byte-exact):** n=89,321 · base 0.1007 · calibrated **AP 0.4974** ·
AUROC 0.8578 · Brier 0.0672 — identical to `expected/operational_benchmark.json`.
**Harness:** `research/bacteria/error_atlas/build_atlas.py` →
`reports/error_atlas/{error_atlas.json, te_deployable.parquet}`.

> Method: reproduced the exact frozen test frame (`--label enterococcus --reveal-lag-days 2`,
> rain + discharge), then sliced calibrated predictions `p_cal` vs the label across 9 operational
> dimensions + an onset/persistence decomposition + a fixed-budget error decomposition.

---

project_docs.title: ERROR ATLAS — Deployable Bacteria Nowcasting Baseline
project_docs.text:
## 1. The one-sentence finding

**The baseline's skill is concentrated where the answer is already obvious (heavy rain,
recently-wet, already-dirty, high-memory beaches); its failures concentrate in a single
coherent blind regime — DRY-WEATHER NEW-ONSET exceedances at normally-clean beaches —
which neither rainfall (the only working driver) nor site memory can see by construction.**

project_docs.title: ERROR ATLAS — Deployable Bacteria Nowcasting Baseline
project_docs.text:
## 2. Where skill is cheap vs where it fails (calibrated AP by slice)

| Dimension | EASY slice (high AP) | HARD slice (low AP) |
|---|---|---|
| Prev state | `prev_dirty` AP **0.660** (lift 1.85) | `prev_clean` AP **0.402** (lift 5.4, **6,022 onset events**) |
| Rain regime | `heavy` AP **0.774** (base 0.425) | `dry` AP **0.300** (**3,678 events**, lift 4.4) |
| Dry spell | `0-1d` since rain AP **0.747** | `22d+` AP **0.285** (2,475 events) |
| Site memory | `high_risk` AP 0.586 | `low_risk` AP **0.233** (lift **8.6×**, 956 surprise events) |
| Season | DJF (wet) AP 0.627 | JJA (dry) AP **0.305** |

Read it as: **the AP 0.497 headline is a blend of an easy wet/persistent mode (AP ~0.66–0.77)
and a hard dry/onset mode (AP ~0.23–0.30).** The wet mode is near-solved; the dry-onset mode is
where ~all remaining signal — if any exists — must come from.

project_docs.text:
# Pipeline Critic Audit — Triaged Findings

**Lead reviewer synthesis** · 2026-06-15 · branch `experimental/bacteria-refactor`

Scope: data ingestion/fetchers, lakehouse build, bacteria core model + baselines, leakage/availability-time, signal-discovery catalog, forecasting/neural models, interlingua transfer, reproduction harness, ops/guards/governance, and a whole-repo bloat sweep. Every finding below was independently verified against the source (file:line) before inclusion. Five additional raised findings were **refuted** on verification and are listed honestly in §5.

---

project_docs.title: Pipeline Critic Audit — Triaged Findings
project_docs.text:
## 1. Executive summary

**The pipeline is fundamentally sound — the science holds, but the *provenance and gates around it leak*.** No CRITICAL defects. There is no finding that invalidates a headline scientific result: the bacteria nowcast still beats every operational baseline, the driver-null still generalizes, and the frozen headline AP reproduces byte-exact when its inputs are present. What the audit found instead is a consistent *class* of problem: **silent, undetected degradation at the edges** — fetchers that can under-fetch without failing, a lakehouse that drops its largest declared source and duplicates 1-in-4 rows with no error, reproduction/integrity gates that fail-closed-silently or fail out-of-the-box, a leave-one-beach-out harness that leaks neighbor labels, and a GPU watchdog that doesn't actually kill the tree.

**Real issues by severity (corrected): 0 critical, 9 high, ~14 medium, ~9 low, plus 5 bloat/deletion candidates.**

**The single most important thing to fix:** the **lakehouse `master_bacteria.parquet` build (`ops/build_lakehouse.py`)** — it silently discards the entire 1.37M-row WQP source AND leaves 25.5% exact-duplicate rows, with no per-source assertion to catch either. Multiple downstream research lanes (CV, spatial impute, semantic discovery, baylens_api) read this "master" table believing it is a unified, deduped statewide corpus. This one file holds two HIGH findings whose blast radius covers every consumer of the silver layer. **Add a per-source row-count assertion and a post-concat `drop_duplicates` + ratio gate, and either backfill WQP geo or drop it from the manifest.**

Cross-cutting theme behind the HIGH items: **add loud assertions where the pipeline currently degrades silently.** Most fixes are small (a sort key, a drop_duplicates, a shared constant import, a taskkill /T), not architectural.

---

project_docs.title: Pipeline Critic Audit — Triaged Findings
project_docs.text:
## 2. CRITICAL & HIGH findings (act now)

No CRITICAL findings. The 9 HIGH findings, ranked by severity then blast radius:

| # | Area | Title | file:line | Category | Why it matters | Fix |
|---|------|-------|-----------|----------|----------------|-----|
| 1 | Lakehouse build | WQP geo-rename silently drops every WQP row | `ops/build_lakehouse.py:75-91, 437-439` | correctness | `load_wqp()` renames non-existent `lat`/`lon` cols → latitude/longitude all-NaN → `dropna(subset=CORE_COLS)` deletes all 1.37M WQP rows. Published master has `{legacy, surfrider}` and **zero wqp**, yet the largest declared statewide FIB source is advertised as included. No error raised. | Backfill WQP geo from `MonitoringLocationIdentifier` (mirror the legacy `station_geo` backfill) before the dropna, or drop `load_wqp()` + remove wqp from the manifest. **Add a per-source row-count assertion so any source going to 0 rows fails the build loud.** |
| 2 | Lakehouse build | No cross-source dedup — 25.5% of silver rows are exact duplicates | `ops/build_lakehouse.py:437-462` | correctness | `pd.concat(dfs)` then only `dropna` — **no `drop_duplicates` anywhere**. Measured 344,191/1,350,892 (25.5%) full-row byte-identical dups (overwhelmingly intra-legacy from an already-dup upstream parquet). Every consumer (`lakehouse_bacteria_cv`, `semantic_shadow_discovery`, `impute_domoic_acid_spatial`, `baylens_api`) trains/evaluates on inflated counts, rolling features, CV folds. | `drop_duplicates` on a stable obs key after concat; assert dedup ratio within an expected bound; source-priority keep order if overlaps are real. |
| 3 | Leakage/causality | LOBO leaks held-out beach labels via KNN neighbor features it never recomputes per fold | `research/bacteria/spatial_autocorr.py:55-74, 113-145, 238-247` | methodology | `nbr_prev1/nbr_prev7` are built globally over ALL beaches once; `leave_one_beach_out` only re-aggregates `cty_prev7/sw_prev7` excluding held-out beaches, never the KNN neighbor state. A held-out beach is a nearest neighbor of training beaches, so its own labels feed training rows. The module's *entire purpose* (unseen-beach AP + Moran's-I proximity check) is contaminated — both the AP is inflated and residual clustering is suppressed. | Recompute `nbr_prev1/nbr_prev7` per fold over non-held-out beaches (mirror `_agg_prev7_excluding`), OR strip spatial feats before the LOBO fit and document it. Apply same discipline to `spatial_holdout.py`'s LOCO path. |
| 4 | Ops/guards | `kill_process_tree()` only kills the direct child — leaks GPU worker processes | `ops/run_safe.py:259-269, 352` | correctness | Popen launched with no `CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP`/`start_new_session`; `terminate()`/`kill()` hit one PID. Wrapper launches (`python -m …`) fork CUDA + DataLoader grandchildren that survive the watchdog "halt", so on a loss-explosion/throughput-collapse the **VRAM is not freed** — the exact OOM this launcher exists to prevent. Sibling `gpu_idle_runner.py:161-189` already does it right. | On win32 launch with `CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP` + kill via `taskkill /PID <pid> /T /F`; POSIX `start_new_session=True` + `os.killpg`. Reuse `gpu_idle_runner.terminate_process_tree`. |
| 5 | Signal catalog | `ecological_state_transition` promoted to KEEP on a label-leaking oracle | `signals/catalog.yaml:497-510` | methodology | KEEP cites "5.44x rain ratio; Oracle AP lift +0.42" but `sharp_test_laneB.py` runs no ΔAP/no LOBO and hard-codes `VERDICT: KEEP`; the "+0.42" comes from `oracle_bound_laneB.py` injecting `y_true` into predictions (perfect-knowledge oracle). The repo's own `critic_laneB.py` FLAGs the same premise ("catastrophic precision"). Pollutes the single-source-of-truth ledger with a fake win, violating its own header rules. | Demote to WASH/UNTESTED until run through `signal_lab.evaluate` over FEATS+rain with LOBO; replace the oracle metric with the gated ΔAP; cite the critic FLAG. Never cite a `y_true`-consuming oracle as a KEEP metric. |
| 6 | Forecasting | Seasonal-naive gate + honest-baseline scorers pin hardcoded v2 cache; producer writes v4 | `ops/seasonal_naive.py:70-71` (also `honest_baseline.py:35-36`, `build_selector.py:29-30`, `build_explorer.py:44`) | reproducibility | `CACHE_VERSION` bumped v2→v3→v4 in the producer but consumers still read `long_v2_*`. If v2 panels are pruned, `load_observed_panel()→None`, `seasonal_naive_table()` returns empty, and per its own docstring the **AGENTS.md-binding best-of{persistence,seasonal-naive} gate silently goes dark** — promotion proceeds on persistence alone. | Import a shared `CACHE_VERSION` constant in all four consumers; add a test asserting `load_observed_panel()` resolves the current version. |
| 7 | Repro harness | `MANIFEST.sha256` fails out-of-the-box — rainfall hashes don't match committed files | `research/bacteria/reproduce/MANIFEST.sha256:3-4` | reproducibility | `sha256sum -c` FAILs for `rainfall_grid.parquet` and `station_grid_map.parquet` at HEAD (manifest carries stale commit-49631e6-era hashes; files + manifest committed together in 337b2d3 but hashes never regenerated). REPRODUCE.md:114-118 tells reviewers to run exactly this check to "verify you have the exact inputs" — i

project_docs.title: Structured disagreement between ddPCR and culture enterococcus at California beaches: a miscalibrated equivalence thresh
project_docs.text:
# Structured disagreement between ddPCR and culture enterococcus at California beaches: a miscalibrated equivalence threshold and a predictable culture blind spot

**Monterey Bay AI Lab — technical report**
**Date:** 2026-06-20
**Status:** Diagnostic / hypothesis-generating. Changes no operational model. Circulated for external scientific review.
**Analysis conducted by:** autonomous-agent-assisted pipeline (Claude), reviewed against an adversarial internal critic. Numbers below are machine-generated from the cited code and are byte-reproducible.

---

project_docs.title: Structured disagreement between ddPCR and culture enterococcus at California beaches: a miscalibrated equivalence thresh
project_docs.text:
## Plain-language summary

California beaches are posted (open/closed) using **culture** enterococcus tests (colony growth, results in ~18–24 h). A faster molecular method, **ddPCR** (droplet digital PCR, results in ~2–4 h, counts gene copies), is being evaluated as a replacement. We have a dataset where **both** assays were run on the **same water sample, same station, same day** (909 paired station-days, San Diego County, 2020–2025).

We asked two questions and got two clean answers:

1. **Do the two tests agree?** At the regulator's official "equivalence" thresholds they agree poorly (they disagree on the swim-advisory call **48%** of the time), and the disagreement is almost entirely one-directional: ddPCR flags contamination ~**4×** more often than culture. But this is **mostly a threshold problem** — the *ranking* of dirty vs clean days agrees very well (AUC 0.90). The official ddPCR cutoff appears set ~**46× too low** relative to the culture exceedance rate.

2. **When the two tests disagree, is it random noise or a real pattern?** It is a **real, predictable pattern.** On days culture calls the water *safe*, we can predict — out-of-station, above chance, with a clean permutation-null — whether ddPCR would have flagged it, using **only season, location, and the site's recent history** (not the same-day culture value). That means there are identifiable beaches and seasons where the culture standard systematically passes water carrying high molecular fecal-marker load.

**What we cannot say:** which test is *right* for public health. ddPCR detects DNA from dead as well as live cells, so a "molecular-positive / culture-negative" day is not proven to be an illness risk. That is the open epidemiological question and the reason we are circulating this.

---

project_docs.title: Structured disagreement between ddPCR and culture enterococcus at California beaches: a miscalibrated equivalence thresh
project_docs.text:
## 1. Background and motivation

This lab has spent a large autonomous-agent campaign trying to improve coastal fecal-indicator-bacteria (FIB) prediction by adding environmental drivers (rainfall, discharge, tides, waves, satellite ocean color, soil moisture, upwelling, etc.). The robust result across dozens of gated experiments is a **"driver-null"**: beyond autocorrelation/persistence (what the beach did last week), new environmental drivers add essentially nothing in held-out average precision. The signal ledger currently stands at ~39 WASH/REJECT vs ~13 mostly-tiny-or-redundant KEEP.

That pushed us to a different axis: **the target label itself.** Regulatory FIB labels come from culture. A prior internal note hypothesized that a lower-noise *molecular* label (ddPCR) would raise the achievable prediction ceiling (the "denoise" hypothesis). A first descriptive analysis (`ddpcr_label_noise.py`) **refuted the naive denoise framing**: ddPCR is not a cleaner read of the same quantity — it is ~4× more positive, i.e. a different target. It deliberately stopped there.

This report answers the question that left open: **is the culture↔ddPCR disagreement a miscalibrated threshold, irreducible noise, or genuine structure?**

project_docs.text:
## Status
**Critic-corrected + B1 operationalized.** An independent critic pass + an independent companion
analysis caught three errors, all corrected in-place: B1 re-anchored to site-memory (shipped
product), B2 kill retracted (frontier reopened), B3 withdrawn, and a "stale data" mistake fixed
(headline reproduces byte-exact, cal AP 0.4974). Shipped artifact:
`research/bacteria/sampling_triage.py` + `tests/test_sampling_triage.py` (3 green) →
`reports/sampling_triage/{triage_eval.json, sampling_priority_latest.csv}`. Net honest deliverable =
**one shipped, gate-backed win (sampling triage, +0.16 capture @20% vs site-memory, CI excl 0) + a
reopened frontier (sub-county real-time regional signal, +0.05–0.06 AP oracle) + a validated Error
Atlas failure map.** Framing gate PASS. Deliverable: `docs/findings/bacteria-error-atlas-companion.md`.
Harnesses (tested by re-run reproducibility): `research/bacteria/error_atlas.py` (per-row test preds
+ slices + triage capture) and `research/bacteria/error_atlas_oracles.py` (frontier oracle bounds).
The Atlas is faithful to the frozen pipeline (the headline reproduces BYTE-EXACT at calibrated
AP 0.4974; an earlier "stale data" claim was wrong — discharge had been omitted and raw vs calibrated
AP compared). Findings below are CRITIC-CORRECTED.

**The story (CRITIC-CORRECTED — an independent critic refuted 2 of 3 findings; integrated):** the
baseline's skill lives in wet/winter/high-prior conditions; the EVENTS live in dry/summer/onset — 82%
of expensive false negatives are dry-AND-prev-clean "surprise" exceedances. This *explains* the
driver-null (washed drivers are rain/season-correlated → orthogonal to the residual). Net after the
critic pass: **(B1 SURVIVES, operationalized)** the deployable value is a sampling-TRIAGE product, but
honestly benchmarked vs SITE MEMORY (the real status quo, not uniform) the lift is **+0.16 capture /
1.3× @20% budget** (1.74× @top-5%) — real and shipped. **(B2 RETRACTED)** the "+0.018 AP spill-feed is
dead" claim used a weak rank-sum combiner; a proper stack gives +0.05–0.06 AP oracle on the dry-onset
slice → the sub-county real-time regional-feed frontier is REOPENED. **(B3 WITHDRAWN)** the "17% label
flip / near-ceiling" claim was apples-to-oranges (confirmatory-resample subset); at weekly cadence the
flip is 8% and the model BEATS the yesterday-value oracle → NO label-noise ceiling near model
performance. **Verdict: ship the triage product (anchored to site-memory); the next real frontier is a
SUB-COUNTY real-time regional/spill signal (oracle headroom +0.05–0.06 AP), tested under LOBO.**

# ERROR ATLAS — deployable-stratum bacteria baseline (companion analysis)

**Date:** 2026-06-15. **Harness:** `research/bacteria/error_atlas.py` →
`reports/error_atlas/{error_atlas.json, test_predictions.parquet}`.

project_docs.title: ERROR ATLAS — deployable-stratum bacteria baseline (companion analysis)
project_docs.text:
## 0. Provenance & reproduction check (read first) — ⚠️CORRECTED
Baseline under audit = `research/bacteria/operational_benchmark.py --label enterococcus
--reveal-lag-days 2`, stratum **EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO** (n=89,321, base 0.1007). **CORRECTION (I was
wrong about "stale data"):** the frozen headline **reproduces BYTE-EXACT — calibrated AP 0.4974**
(verified: `--rain-dir --discharge-dir` → raw 0.5119 / **calibrated 0.4974**). My earlier "0.0107
gap = rainfall drift" was TWO mistakes: (1) my atlas harness omitted the discharge features, and
(2) it reports the **raw** p_model AP (0.5081 rain-only / 0.5119 with discharge) and I compared it to
the **calibrated** headline number — isotonic regression introduces score TIES that lower AP-with-ties
(0.5119→0.4974). The data is NOT stale; the headline is valid (confirmed by an independent byte-exact
cross-check). **Crucially this does NOT affect the error analysis:** isotonic is monotonic,
so per-row RANKING (hence every slice's relative failure structure and all capture@budget curves) is
identical raw-vs-calibrated; discharge moves calibrated AP only +0.004. The Atlas conclusions stand.
**This is descriptive error analysis of the frozen model — NOT test-set mining for a new signal.**

---

project_docs.title: ERROR ATLAS — deployable-stratum bacteria baseline (companion analysis)
project_docs.text:
## 1. THE HEADLINE FAILURE STRUCTURE (where skill lives vs. where events live)

The baseline's skill is **concentrated in wet / winter / high-prior conditions**, but the **events are
concentrated in dry / summer / onset conditions** — a near-perfect anti-alignment. The model is
weakest exactly where it matters operationally (summer beach use; surprise onset).

| Slice | n | events (% of all 8,999) | model AP | model−station-memory |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| **rain >25mm** | 3,638 | 2,111 (23%) | **0.85** | +0.11 |
| rain dry (≤0.01mm) | 52,038 | 3,541 (39%) | **0.31** | +0.08 |
| antecedent **>14 dry days** | 48,194 | 2,865 (32%) | **0.29** | +0.06 |
| month Feb (wettest) | 6,952 | 1,403 | **0.70** | +0.32 |
| month **Jun (peak beach)** | 8,036 | 434 | **0.23** | +0.04 |
| prev sample **clean (onset)** | 80,951 | **6,022 (67%)** | **0.41** | +0.21 |
| prev sample dirty | 8,348 | 2,971 (33%) | 0.67 | +0.19 |

**Intersections (the operationally expensive core):**
- **dry & onset = 3,364 events = 37% of ALL exceedances.** The single biggest hard mass.
- **dry & summer & onset = 1,710 events = 19% of all exceedances**, sitting in a slice that is
  **46% of the entire stratum (n=41,323), base 4.1%, model AP only 0.181.** Nearly half of all
  station-days fall in a regime where the model is near-blind in absolute precision.

**Operational errors at a 20%-false-alarm budget** (threshold on calibrated p; recall 0.78, 1,990 FN,
18,021 FP):
- **93.3% of false negatives occur when the previous sample was CLEAN** (onset/transition misses).
- **88.1% of false negatives occur on dry days** (>3 d since rain).
- **82.3% of false negatives are BOTH dry AND prev-clean** — a *surprise dry-weather onset* at a site
  that looked clean, with no rain trigger and no recent dirty history. The model has **no information
  to catch these**; they are the dominant expensive error and they are NOT rain-correlated.

project_docs.title: Bacteria Error Atlas — failure structure of the deployable nowcast (operational reframe)
project_docs.text:
## Methodology note (earned this run)
Before declaring a frozen benchmark STALE/DRIFTED, reproduce BOTH the raw AND calibrated metric
columns. Isotonic/Platt calibration lowers a ranking metric (AP) via tie plateaus, so comparing a
freshly-computed RAW AP to a stored CALIBRATED AP masquerades as data drift and can wrongly retire a
valid frozen result. Match the column you are comparing. (In this run, a companion analysis
concluded `expected/` 0.4974 was stale because its raw 0.5081 didn't match — but the expected raw is
0.5119 and reproduces byte-exact; no drift existed.)

# International FIB transfer — research findings log (2026-06-14)

Research log for the international fecal-indicator-bacteria (FIB) transfer campaign: does the
California bacteria nowcast transfer to other regions, and does a pooled multi-region model beat
region-local models? Findings below are recorded with evidence and reproduction paths; each
predictive claim was independently re-computed before it was trusted.

project_docs.title: International FIB transfer — research findings log (2026-06-14)
project_docs.text:
## Ground rules (binding)

- **Value-gate every new thing.** Cite a falsifiable payload, or name the kill-category.
- **Driver-null is the prior.** Most new sources/signals are WASH/REJECT here. The deliverable is
  the *honest verdict* (lift OR null), adversarially verified — not a pile of models. Assume a
  first win is a bug until refuted.
- **Honest baseline, stratified.** Bacteria headline = EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO AP 0.50 / AUC 0.86,
  calibrated ECE 0.02, 9/9 LOCO (`research/bacteria/reproduce/expected/`). Never cite 0.888.
- **GPU through the gate.** Heavy/neural runs use `ops/run_safe.py`.

# International FIB transfer — independent verification log (2026-06-14)

Companion research log to the international FIB transfer campaign: an independent re-implementation
(different loader, features, and code) built to red-team the transfer headline, plus the
professional-tier evaluation harness and QA spine the campaign ran results through. The goal was to
try to kill the transfer result before trusting it.

project_docs.title: International FIB transfer — independent verification log (2026-06-14)
project_docs.text:
## Deliverables

1. `research/bacteria/eval_report.py` — the single reusable full diagnostic block (confusion matrix,
   threshold analysis via Youden-J + max-F1, calibration curve, full stat breakdown, baseline
   comparison with bootstrap CI / permutation significance). Every headline flows through it so all
   results meet the same bar. Tests: `tests/test_eval_report.py` (8/8 pass).
2. Validated + contract-tested the international FIB silver spine
   (`lakehouse/silver/intl_bacteria_observations`) so transfer code pulls deterministically.
3. QA/consistency gate on the expanded corpus (unit bugs, coverage) —
   `ops/source_consistency_check.py`.
4. Adversarial red team of the transfer headline (leakage, base-rate illusion, label-proxy
   mismatch, site overlap, split) before it shipped.

project_docs.title: International FIB transfer — independent verification log (2026-06-14)
project_docs.text:
## Red-team findings

- **Label mismatch across the transfer boundary.** The Ireland label is `ent>185 | ecoli>500`
  (EU directive); the CA headline model trains on `ent>104` (US EPA marine BAV). IE base rate is
  ~identical either way (ent>104 = 0.0342; ent>185 | ecoli>500 = 0.0341) BUT the two definitions
  disagree on ~482 rows — ~58% of the positive events are different rows. Recommendation adopted:
  report the zero-shot regime against the harmonized ent>104 IE label (pure transfer), and keep
  ent>185 | ecoli>500 for the Ireland-local regime where EU regulatory relevance is the right call.
  State that the two labels disagree on ~half the positives.
- **Calibration must be local.** CA base rate ~10% vs IE ~3.4%. A CA-trained model's raw
  probabilities are miscalibrated on IE by construction; quote ECE only after isotonic recalibration
  fit on an IE calibration window, and separate DISCRIMINATION (AP/ROC-AUC, calibration-free) from
  CALIBRATION in the headline so "skill transfers" is not conflated with "probabilities transfer."
- **Model class.** The CA headline is HGBT + isotonic, not RF/LogReg. For a faithful "does the CA
  *model* transfer" claim, an HGBT arm is most representative; RF/LogReg is a robustness ensemble.
- **SOTA bar to cite:** ES&T 2025 "ML, Generalization, and Transfer Learning for FIB threshold
  exceedance at beaches" — source→target specificity 0.70–0.81, sensitivity 0.28–0.76. Report
  sens/spec at a Youden-J operating point for direct comparability.
